BOLT.notes:
PICK #516 NEXT — [PERF-PDP-LCP-P1] Hoist PDP hero preload above Suspense. ROOT CAUSE (established in C4867+C4868 via curl-diff + WebSearch): gallery in src/components/shop/product-gallery.tsx:393 calls ReactDOM.preload() at component-render time, but the gallery renders INSIDE a Suspense boundary that streams AFTER flushes → preload never lands in document , so the browser discovers the hero image only during Suspense chunk render. Result: resourceLoadDelay 3206ms on sold-overlay path, PDP mobile LCP 4.59s (target ≤2.5s). CANONICAL FIX PATH (PREFERRED — metadata.other): in the PDP server component src/app/(shop)/products/[slug]/page.tsx, resolve the first-image URL during server render (already available — product.images[0]) and inject a via generateMetadata's `other` field OR via a direct element rendered ABOVE the that wraps the gallery. Both strategies place the preload in the initial document chunk (which flushes before any Suspense boundary streams). ALTERNATIVE FIX PATH (ReactDOM.preload hoist): import ReactDOM from 'react-dom' and call ReactDOM.preload(firstImageUrl, {as: 'image', fetchPriority: 'high'}) in the PDP server component BEFORE the boundary — Next 16 hoists this to . Pick whichever is cleaner; metadata.other tends to be more idiomatic for static preloads, ReactDOM.preload for dynamic. DO NOT also leave `priority` on the gallery's first (Next 16 deprecated it → rename to `preload` on the Image component per #518 Row S remaining sites if you touch that line). DO NOT change gallery's Suspense wrapping (that would be a separate refactor). DO NOT preload any image other than the first frame. DO NOT preload on sold overlays specifically — preload the actual first image unconditionally, sold overlay will compose over it. TEST CRITERIA: `curl -sI -A 'Mozilla/5.0' https://jvsatnik.cz/products/ | head -40` then `curl -s -A 'Mozilla/5.0' https://jvsatnik.cz/products/ | grep -i 'rel=\"preload\"\|rel=preload' | head` MUST show the hero image URL with fetchPriority=high BEFORE /Suspense-streamed content, on BOTH a live PDP AND a sold PDP. Commit fmt: 'Cycle #NNNN: Bolt — Task #516 [PERF-PDP-LCP-P1]: hoisted PDP hero preload via metadata.other (or ReactDOM.preload) above Suspense boundary. Head now emits before on live+sold PDPs (verified curl). tsc 0 err, eslint clean (milestone 31 consecutive), next build green.'. Target ~20-40 LoC, single atomic commit. AFTER #516 LANDS → #517 [PERF-CSS-BLOCK] retire the 32KB render-blocking CSS chunk (300ms tax on every mobile route): (a) identify the chunk (likely a globals.css emission that exceeds Next's critical-CSS inlining threshold), (b) split non-critical rules (below-the-fold animations, admin-only classes leaked into shop layout, unused tailwind variants) into dynamic imports or @media queries, (c) target: render-blocking CSS ≤14KB (HTTP/2 initcwnd). Atomic commit. AFTER #517 LANDS → #515 [PERF-COOKIE-LCP-P2] cookie banner non-LCP strategy (options a/b/c from C4868: reduce

viewport footprint / fetchpriority=high on real hero / CSS contain:strict + transform on banner). One option per probe if needed, but prefer combining (a)+(c) since they're low-risk. Atomic commit. ALL THREE SHIP SERIALLY (not bundled) so #484 Lighthouse replay can attribute LCP/CLS/INP deltas per-task. After #515 lands → Bolt IDLE pending #484 verify outcome + new backlog items (Row S 6 inline caches cacheTag wiring is NEXT after the perf bundle; do NOT bundle into #516/#517/#515). Do NOT touch #513 cart page — that's shipped and waits on verify. Do NOT regress lint 0w/0e milestone (currently 30 consecutive, target 33 after #515).

SAGE.notes: UNCHANGED from C4868 — PICK #494 Phase 3 NEXT. Phase 1 SHIPPED (9c23a71): layout.ts + 10 templates via renderLayout. Phase 2 SHIPPED (tracked). PHASE 3 SCOPE: (a) Add optional `showUnsubscribe: boolean` arg to renderLayout so marketing emails can render an unsubscribe footer while transactional skip it. (b) Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the recipient email (+ optional campaign id) using `createHmac('sha256', env.UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET)` in src/lib/email/unsubscribe.ts (new file), generate `/unsubscribe?email=<email>&sig=<hex>` link, inject into renderLayout footer when showUnsubscribe=true. The HTTP /unsubscribe route stub itself is OUT OF SCOPE (separate Bolt task) — only the link generation lands in this phase. (c) Wire showUnsubscribe=true at all marketing-class renderLayout call-sites (campaign, newsletter, welcome-variant, browse-abandonment, cross-sell, win-back, MOTHERS_DAY/CUSTOMS builders) and =false/omitted at transactional (admin-new-order, review-request, delivery-check, new-arrival). (d) IN PARALLEL — Row Q dingbat purge: replace 7 numeric HTML entities `&#[0-9]+;` in src/lib/email.ts, src/lib/email/layout.ts, src/lib/email/wishlist-sold.ts with their UTF-8 literals (grep -rn '&#[0-9]\+;' src/lib/email* to confirm the 7 sites). Commit fmt: 'Cycle #NNNN: Sage — Task #494 Phase 3: HMAC unsubscribe footer via UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET + createHmac + showUnsubscribe bool on renderLayout; Row Q dingbats purged. tsc 0 err, eslint clean (milestone preserved), build green.'. May bundle HMAC + dingbats into ONE commit if diff stays <150 LoC; split if larger. DO NOT touch smtp-transport.ts / addresses.ts. DO NOT re-add Resend. DO NOT write the /unsubscribe route handler yet (bectly gate on UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET env var).
